misconceptionSummary: Students put values into the wrong symbols or treat all letters in the formula as the same thing.

steps.narration: This substitution is wrong. The width value has been ignored and the length value has been used twice.
Each value must go into the matching symbol only. L gets seven and w gets four.
Now the formula has been substituted correctly.
Now calculate. Seven times four equals twenty-eight.

visuals.expression: Match each given value to the correct symbol
visuals.label: Do not reuse one value for every letter

steps.narration: To avoid the mistake, read the symbol names carefully before inserting any values.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: substituting one given number into every letter in the formula. Each symbol has its own value and meaning.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: A formula only works if each quantity is matched to the correct symbol.
